key_insight: PDAce redesigns primal-dual updates for nonlinear saddle-point problems by using convex-combination points and mapping-space extrapolation, removing nonlinear residuals and enabling clean convergence analysis without linesearch.
engineering_relevance: This gives a more stable and theoretically grounded solver template for optimization tasks in signal processing, machine learning, robust optimization, and generative modeling with nonlinear coupling.
method: Linearize the nonlinear coupling term at a convex-combination point instead of the raw iterate.
Shift extrapolation from variable space to the nonlinear mapping space using the latest convex-combination information.
Use a Lyapunov potential to prove global convergence for PDAce and accelerated rates for aPDAce under stronger convexity.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports theoretical guarantees: PDAce achieves ergodic O(1/N) convergence, aPDAce achieves O(1/N^2) under strong convexity of the primal term, and linear convergence when both sides are strongly convex.
watch_item: The method’s practical performance may depend on how well the convex-combination step and Jacobian evaluations are computed for large-scale nonlinear models.
paper_title: A New Primal-Dual Algorithm with Convex Combination and Extrapolation for Convex-Concave Saddle Point Problems with Nonlinear Coupling Term
